title: The Growing Global Challenge of Cancer Care
subtitle: New report highlights how unequal access to treatment is creating a worldwide health crisis.

summary.en: A new report shows that cancer cases are rising quickly around the world. While science has made great progress, many people still cannot get the care they need. Wealthy countries have much higher survival rates for common cancers than poorer nations. Experts warn that the cost of treatment is causing serious financial and emotional problems for families. Governments are being urged to prioritize both prevention and affordable care to close these gaps.
summary.zh: 一份最新報告顯示，全球癌症病例正迅速增加。儘管科學已取得巨大進步，許多人仍無法獲得所需的照護。富裕國家在常見癌症的存活率上遠高於貧窮國家。專家警告，治療費用正為家庭帶來嚴重的經濟與心理問題。各國政府受敦促應優先考慮預防措施與負擔得起的醫療照護，以縮小這些差距。

paragraphs.en: Scientific progress has brought new hope to many patients, but a new report highlights that this progress has not reached everyone equally. According to The Guardian Health, while new technologies and treatments are changing the story of cancer, millions of people still face difficult physical, emotional, and financial challenges after a diagnosis. The report warns that the world is seeing persistent and widening gaps in how people access prevention, diagnosis, and medical care.
paragraphs.zh: 科學進步為許多病患帶來了新希望，但一份最新報告指出，這些進步並未平等地惠及所有人。根據《衛報健康》（The Guardian Health）報導，儘管新技術與療法正在改寫癌症的治療歷程，但仍有數百萬人在確診後，面臨艱鉅的生理、心理與經濟挑戰。該報告警告，全球在預防、診斷及醫療照護的取得管道上，正出現持續且不斷擴大的差距。

paragraphs.en: Experts estimate that one in five people will develop cancer during their lifetime. The disease is expected to touch 92% of the population, either through a personal diagnosis or through a close family member. Currently, there are about 20.6 million new cases and 10 million deaths from cancer every year. If current trends continue, The Guardian Health reports that these figures are projected to rise to nearly 35 million cases by 2050.
paragraphs.zh: 專家估計，每五人中就有一人在一生中會罹患癌症。預計有百分之九十二的人口會受到此疾病影響，無論是親自確診，或是身邊有親近的家人罹病。目前，全球每年約有兩千零六十萬起新增病例，以及一千萬人死於癌症。若目前的趨勢持續下去，《衛報健康》指出，預計到二〇五〇年，這些數字將攀升至近三千五百萬起病例。

paragraphs.en: The gap between rich and poor nations is particularly concerning. In wealthier countries, 85% of people diagnosed with breast or childhood cancers will survive for at least five years. However, that number drops to less than 30% in poorer countries. Access to medicine is also very different. In high-income countries, patients can access between 68% and 94% of the top-20 priority cancer drugs. In contrast, in low- and lower-middle-income countries, that availability falls to between 9% and 54%. Furthermore, the report found that 23 countries have no radiation facilities at all, which makes effective treatment nearly impossible for many.
paragraphs.zh: 貧富國家之間的差距尤其令人擔憂。在較富裕的國家，百分之八十五的乳癌或兒童癌症患者能存活至少五年；然而，在較貧窮的國家，這一比例降至不到百分之三十。藥物的取得管道也大不相同。在高收入國家，患者能取得百分之六十八至九十四的前二十大優先癌症藥物；相比之下，在低收入及中低收入國家，藥物取得率僅剩百分之九至五十四。此外，報告發現有二十三個國家完全沒有放射治療設施，這使得許多患者幾乎無法獲得有效的治療。

paragraphs.en: Financial hardship is a major barrier to care. The Guardian Health notes that two-thirds of countries do not include cancer treatment in their universal health coverage—a system where the government pays for healthcare for all citizens. Because of these high costs, up to 90% of patients in some areas are forced to stop their treatment. Abigail Simon-Hart, a breast cancer survivor and patient advocate from Nigeria, shared that she has seen families forced to choose between paying for life-saving medicine and keeping their children in school. In some cases, children have to leave school entirely because all family resources are spent on cancer care.
paragraphs.zh: 經濟困難是醫療照護的一大阻礙。《衛報健康》指出，三分之二的國家未將癌症治療納入全民健康覆蓋範圍，即政府為所有公民支付醫療費用的制度。由於治療費用高昂，在某些地區，高達百分之九十的患者被迫中斷治療。來自奈及利亞的乳癌倖存者兼病患權益倡議者艾比蓋兒·賽門-哈特（Abigail Simon-Hart）分享道，她曾見過許多家庭被迫在支付救命藥物與讓孩子繼續上學之間做出抉擇。在某些案例中，孩子甚至必須完全輟學，因為家庭的所有資源都耗費在癌症照護上。

paragraphs.en: Beyond financial issues, social stigma also plays a dangerous role. Simon-Hart explained that in some places, the fear of social judgment is so strong that women choose to die rather than undergo life-saving surgeries like mastectomies, which involve removing breast tissue. This stigma can be deadly, as it prevents people from seeking the help they need early enough for treatment to be successful.
paragraphs.zh: 除了經濟問題，社會汙名化也扮演了危險的角色。賽門-哈特解釋，在某些地方，對社會輿論的恐懼非常強烈，以至於女性寧願選擇死亡，也不願接受如乳房切除術（切除乳房組織）等救命手術。這種汙名化可能致命，因為它阻礙了人們在治療能發揮成效的早期階段尋求必要的協助。

paragraphs.en: Despite these grim statistics, there are some reasons for optimism. The report highlights that there is a clear path to eliminating cervical cancer, and many countries are seeing a downward trend in tobacco use. Additionally, most nations now have national plans to take action against cancer. Dr. Isabelle Soerjomataram, a researcher who worked on the report, noted that four out of every ten new cancer cases are linked to risk factors that we already know how to address. These include tobacco use, alcohol consumption, infections, and maintaining a healthy body weight.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管數據嚴峻，仍有一些值得樂觀的理由。報告強調，目前已有明確的路徑可以消除子宮頸癌，且許多國家的菸草使用率正呈現下降趨勢。此外，大多數國家現在都有應對癌症的國家級計畫。參與該報告的研究員伊莎貝爾·索喬馬塔蘭博士（Dr. Isabelle Soerjomataram）指出，每十起新增癌症病例中，就有四起與我們已知如何處理的風險因子有關，包括菸草使用、酒精攝取、感染以及維持健康的體重。

paragraphs.en: To address these challenges, experts are calling on the global community to value care as much as they value finding a cure. They are urging governments to provide more funding for cancer services, covering everything from early prevention to final treatment. By focusing on these areas, leaders hope to reduce the number of cases and ensure that a cancer diagnosis does not lead to financial ruin or a lack of hope for patients around the world.
paragraphs.zh: 為了應對這些挑戰，專家呼籲全球社會應像重視尋求治癒方法一樣，重視照護服務。他們敦促各國政府為癌症服務提供更多資金，涵蓋從早期預防到最終治療的所有環節。透過聚焦於這些領域，各國領袖希望能減少病例數量，並確保全球各地的患者不會因為確診癌症而陷入經濟崩潰或失去希望。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To affect or involve someone or something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（觸摸），這裡指對人產生影響。文中：The disease is expected to touch 92% of the population, either through a personal diagnosis or through a close family member.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A significant difference or inequality between two groups or situations.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（縫隙），這裡指社會或經濟上的差距。文中：The report warns that the world is seeing persistent and widening gaps in how people access prevention, diagnosis, and medical care.

keyTerms.definitionEn: The state of being completely destroyed or having lost all money.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（毀壞），這裡作名詞使用。文中：By focusing on these areas, leaders hope to reduce the number of cases and ensure that a cancer diagnosis does not lead to financial ruin or a lack of hope for patients around the world.

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: Cancer cases expected to soar worldwide, WHO report finds

quiz.question: According to the report, what is the primary reason why many patients in low-income countries cannot receive effective cancer treatment?

options.A: They lack the motivation to seek medical help.
options.B: They face a severe shortage of essential infrastructure like radiation facilities.
options.C: They are choosing to prioritize their children's education over their own health.
options.D: They have access to 94% of priority cancer drugs but cannot afford them.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，報告明確指出有 23 個國家完全沒有放射治療設施，這使得許多患者幾乎無法獲得有效的治療。選項 A 文中未提及；選項 C 是患者面臨的困境之一，而非缺乏治療設施的直接原因；選項 D 錯誤，因為 94% 的藥物獲取率是針對高收入國家，而非低收入國家。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the relationship between universal health coverage and cancer treatment?

options.A: Universal health coverage is currently the standard for cancer care in most countries.
options.B: Without universal health coverage, cancer treatment is often financially unsustainable for families.
options.C: Universal health coverage ensures that all cancer patients have access to radiation facilities.
options.D: Governments that provide universal health coverage have successfully eliminated cancer stigma.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文章第四段提到三分之二的國家未將癌症治療納入全民健保，且由於費用高昂，高達 90% 的患者被迫中斷治療，這暗示了缺乏公共財政支持會導致家庭面臨嚴重的財務崩潰。選項 A 與文中提到的「三分之二國家未納入」矛盾；選項 C 文中並未保證健保一定包含放射設施；選項 D 則與社會污名化問題無直接關聯。

quiz.question: In the phrase 'The disease is expected to touch 92% of the population,' what does the word 'touch' most likely mean?

options.B: To affect or involve someone's life.
options.C: To create a small amount of something.
options.D: To reach a specific physical location.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。在此語境中，「touch」並非指物理上的觸碰，而是指疾病對個人生活造成的影響（透過個人確診或親友確診）。選項 A 是字面意思，但在這裡不適用；選項 C 與 D 皆與文中討論的疾病影響範圍無關，屬於錯誤的語意延伸。

quiz.question: What is the central message of this article regarding global cancer care?

options.A: Scientific progress has successfully made cancer treatment equal for everyone.
options.B: The only way to solve the cancer crisis is to find a universal cure.
options.C: Cancer is primarily a problem of wealthy nations that have better diagnostic tools.
options.D: Closing the gap in cancer care requires addressing financial, social, and structural inequalities.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。全文旨在探討全球癌症照護的巨大不平等，並強調解決方案需涵蓋財務支持（健保）、社會觀念（污名化）以及醫療基礎設施（放射治療）。選項 A 與文章開頭提到的「進步並未平等惠及所有人」直接衝突；選項 B 與最後一段專家呼籲「重視照護如同重視治療」的觀點不符；選項 C 忽略了疾病對全球的普遍影響。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
